body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: evaluator consolidation
body.intention: I intend that the predicted effects of evaluator consolidation are observed — specifically whether tool use decreases and verdicts appear on tickets.
body.description:
**What changed:** Critic/Improver/Judge consolidated around EvaluatorCore with optimism parameter; verdicts stamped to ticket metadata post-sprint.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- EvaluatorCore inference latency adds time to every Critic call inline in DickSimnel's tool loop — could slow sprints noticeably
- Optimism bias may be too coarse — -1/+1 may not produce meaningfully different verdicts if the rubric overrides the bias
- Improver rules persisted to disk may accumulate stale rules that conflict with updated patterns
- Post-sprint verdict stamping on ticket metadata may conflict with other metadata writers (race condition at close time)
- CriticDevice refactor may subtly change verdict quality — DickSimnel may make different decisions, for better or worse

**Signals to watch:** Tool call count per sprint in logs (expect decrease); verdict fields visible via cc_queue.py show; eval_history() returning entries; sprint duration before/after.

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-28 or when T-judge-wire-post-sprint closes.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — observation ticket
